query: Ilya Sutskever 2026 Musk v Altman trial OpenAI stake amount testimony source

{"query":"Ilya Sutskever 2026 Musk v Altman trial OpenAI stake amount testimony source","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1684,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Ilya Sutskever Testifies He Holds $7 Billion OpenAI Stake—Second New Billionaire Revealed In Musk-Altman Trial.May 11, 2026"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/aliciapark/2026/05/11/ilya-sutskever-testifies-he-holds-7-billion-openai-stake-second-new-billionaire-revealed-in-musk-altman-trial/#:~:text=Ilya%20Sutskever%20Testifies%20He%20Holds,Revealed%20In%20Musk%2DAltman%20Trial","title":"Ilya Sutskever Testifies He Holds $7 Billion OpenAI Stake—Second New Billionaire Revealed In Musk-Altman Trial","content":"Sutskever, who worked as a research scientist at Google for two years, testified Google offered him $6 million a year to stay at the company instead of leaving to found OpenAI—a "staggering amount of money" to him at the time, he said.
Sutskever, who immigrated from Russia to Israel as a child and later to Canada, earned his PhD in computer science at University of Toronto before joining Google as a research scientist.
Sutskever joins Brockman as the second OpenAI billionaire to be revealed during the trial.
Former chief technology officer Mira Murati, who testified in the trial last week, launched Thinking Machines Lab, which closed a staggering $2 billion seed round last June at roughly a $12 billion valuation, with a team that's two-thirds OpenAI alumni including cofounder John Schulman and former research director Barret Zoph."},{"url":"https://observer.com/2026/05/ilya-sutskever-revisits-openai-ouster/","title":"Ilya Sutskever Details OpenAI Power Struggle in Musk-Altman Lawsuit | Observer","content":"Altman, who was ultimately restored as OpenAI’s CEO, now faces allegations from another co-founder, Musk.
His shares, valued at around $5 billion in November, are now worth roughly $7 billion, he said in testimony.
OpenAI, now one of the world’s most valuable startups with an $852 billion valuation, is preparing for a highly anticipated IPO that could reportedly take place in the second half of 2026."},{"url":"https://www.businessinsider.com/musk-altman-openai-trial-final-questions-remain-sutskever-2026-5","title":"The Musk-Altman Trial Is in the Final Stretch. These Big Questions Still Remain. - Business Insider","content":"These big questions still remain.
OpenAI cofounder and former executive Ilya Sutskever, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, and Altman himself are all expected to testify."},{"url":"https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/sy00kqxljgg","title":"Ilya Sutskever reveals $7 billion OpenAI fortune during trial | Ctech","content":"Ilya Sutskever testified on Monday that his stake in OpenAI is now worth about $7 billion.
Sutskever, one of the most influential researchers in artificial intelligence and a central figure in Sam Altman’s brief ouster from OpenAI in 2023, appeared in a California courtroom during the third week of Elon Musk’s lawsuit against the company and its leadership.
During his testimony, Sutskever said he had spent roughly a year collecting evidence for OpenAI’s board that he believed showed a “consistent pattern of lying” by CEO Sam Altman.
He said the concerns included Altman “undermining and pitting executives against one another,” conduct that Sutskever argued was “not conducive to any grand goal,” including the safe development of artificial general intelligence.
Sutskever said his holdings in the company were worth approximately $5 billion in November 2025 and around $7 billion today.
Yet despite helping orchestrate Altman’s removal, Sutskever described himself as largely detached from the public fallout that followed."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cg7pj8p5mv4o","title":"Musk v Altman - 5 big moments in the tech bros' OpenAI trial","content":"Witnesses included OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever, former OpenAI board member, Tasha McCauley, and even Microsoft boss Satya Nadella - who insisted his company did extensive due diligence before investing billions into OpenAI.
Altman was, until recently, chairman of Helion's board and holds a stake worth more than $1.5bn.
He described Zilis, an executive at his firm Neuralink, as one of his senior advisors."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Ilya Sutskever 2026 Musk v Altman trial OpenAI stake amount testimony source","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/aliciapark/2026/05/11/ilya-sutskever-testifies-he-holds-7-billion-openai-stake-second-new-billionaire-revealed-in-musk-altman-trial","title":"Ilya Sutskever Testifies He Holds $7 Billion OpenAI Stake—Second New Billionaire Revealed In Musk-Altman Trial","content":"# Ilya Sutskever Testifies He Holds $7 Billion OpenAI Stake—Second New Billionaire Revealed In Musk-Altman Trial. OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever confirmed Monday a $7 billion stake in OpenAI during his testimony for the high-stakes trial between Elon Musk and the ChatGPT maker, marking the second newly revealed OpenAI billionaire after president Greg Brockman testified a near $30 billion stake last week. Sutskever, who worked as a research scientist at Google for two years, testified Google offered him $6 million a year to stay at the company instead of leaving to found OpenAI—a \"staggering amount of money\" to him at the time, he said. On questions about the board’s firing of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, Sutskever acknowledged that he had been thinking about the issues surrounding Altman for at least a year leading up to the firing,. Brockman testified last week that he and Sutskever denied Musk a majority stake in OpenAI during a meeting in 2017, recalling that Musk was so angry that he thought Musk would become physically aggressive.","score":0.88822687,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://observer.com/2026/05/ilya-sutskever-revisits-openai-ouster","title":"Ilya Sutskever Details OpenAI Power Struggle in Musk-Altman Lawsuit | Observer","content":"## A high-stakes trial reveals new details about OpenAI’s power struggle, including former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever’s pivotal role in Altman’s firing in 2023. Ilya Sutskever, a former OpenAI executive and co-founder, now has a roughly $7 billion stake in the company. The relationship between Sam Altman and Ilya Sutskever, an OpenAI co-founder and the company’s former chief scientist, has long been tumultuous. He later reversed course and called for Altman’s reinstatement, a move he described as a “Hail Mary” during testimony yesterday (May 11) in the ongoing trial of Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI. Musk is seeking the removal of Altman and OpenAI president Greg Brockman, along with the transfer of up to $180 billion in damages from OpenAI’s for-profit arm to its nonprofit parent. At the time, he was working at Google but left the company—turning down a $6 million salary—to join the fledgling startup, he confirmed in testimony.","score":0.8803145,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://chatgptiseatingtheworld.com/2026/05/12/musk-v-altman-trial-week-3-ilya-sutskever-provides-captivating-testimony-favorable-to-openai","title":"Musk v. Altman trial week 3: Ilya Sutskever provides captivating testimony favorable to OpenAI – Chat GPT Is Eating the World","content":"**Week 3 of the Musk v. **Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella testified about the company’s investment in OpenAI, initially $1 billion, of which Nadella said he was proud to be the first company to back OpenAI.** Nadella also testified about the “chaos” ensuing the attempted firing of Sam Altman and Nadella’s response. **But the star witness of the day was former co-founder of OpenAI Ilya Sutskever.**.In memorable testimony, Sutskever provided compelling explanations for OpenAI’s overall mission and why it needed more funding to achieve it. Sutskever said he made no promise to Musk that OpenAI would remain a nonprofit: “I made no such promise. > OpenAI's lawyer Sarah Eddy asks Ilya Sutskever if he ever made a promise to Elon Musk that OpenAI would remain a nonprofit. Sutskever also revealed that he had been collecting negative information about CEO Sam Altman for a year at the request of the board of directors (for the later termination attempt), and that OpenAI made a brief overture to Anthropic during the period of Altman’s brief termination for possible acquisition or absorption by Anthropic.","score":0.8793233,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.wired.com/story/ilya-sutskever-testifies-musk-v-altman-trial","title":"Ilya Sutskever Stands by His Role in Sam Altman’s OpenAI Ouster: ‘I Didn’t Want It to Be Destroyed’ | WIRED","content":"# Ilya Sutskever Stands by His Role in Sam Altman’s OpenAI Ouster: ‘I Didn’t Want It to Be Destroyed’. The former OpenAI chief scientist may be estranged from the company, but he still came to its defense as he testified on Monday. Elon Musk’s trial against OpenAI and Microsoft entered its final stretch on Monday, with testimony from Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella, former OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever, and current OpenAI chairman Bret Taylor. Sutskever drew the spotlight, revealing an ownership stake in OpenAI’s $850-billion for-profit arm that is currently worth about $7 billion. Earlier in the trial, OpenAI president Greg Brockman acknowledged for the first time that he has around $30 billion worth of OpenAI shares. Brockman said he and Sutskever were “joined at the hip,” until Sutskever helped lead Sam Altman’s brief removal as OpenAI CEO in 2023. (He left and formed a competing AI lab in 2024.) “I felt a great deal of ownership of OpenAI,” he said at one point Monday.","score":0.838376,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/business/former-openai-executive-sutskever-discloses-nearly-7-billion-stake-ai-firm-2026-05-11","title":"Ex-OpenAI exec Sutskever says he spent a year gathering proof of alleged Altman dishonesty","content":"Image 2: Trial in Elon Musk's lawsuit over OpenAI for-profit conversion at a federal courthouse in Oakland. Item 1 of 3 OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever walks outside a U.S. federal courthouse as the trial in Elon Musk's lawsuit over OpenAI's for-profit conversion continues, in Oakland, California, U.S., May 11, 2026. **[1/3]**OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever walks outside a U.S. federal courthouse as the trial in Elon Musk's lawsuit over OpenAI's for-profit conversion continues, in Oakland, California, U.S., May 11, 2026. May 11 (Reuters) - Former OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever testified on Monday that he spent about a year gathering evidence for the ChatGPT maker's board that CEO Sam Altman had displayed a \"consistent pattern ​of lying.\". Sutskever said he had prepared a document gathering evidence of Altman's dishonesty at the request of OpenAI's board, and confirmed Altman's conduct included \"undermining and pitting executives against one another.\" He said he ​had discussed removing Altman with then chief technology officer Mira Murati after the two discussed Altman's behavior \"for a long time.\". *   Reuters and AI, opens new tab.","score":0.8055255,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.47,"request_id":"f3a9bea6-d725-4d67-a3f3-fb65b0273027"}
